title: higginsia verticillata
intro: Higginsia verticillata, also known as the hairy bryozoan, is a type of marine bryozoan, a small, colonial animal that is attached to a rock or other underwater surface by a stalk-like structure. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Higginsia verticillata is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antiviral activities. Herbal preparations of Higginsia verticillata are typically made by extracting its bioactive compounds from the colony, which can be used in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams.
